Provides LSP support for a set of web languages.

The plugin enables the following features in Smart Mode:
- Code completion
- Error detection
- Documentation
- Navigation to usages, definitions, types, and implementations

To enable the plugin, please set the `ff.weblsp.enabled` property to `true` in your `settings.json`. This change requires IDE restart.
Upon the first opening of a web project with this flag on, the according language server artifacts will be downloaded.
To enable inlay variable hints for TypeScript server, please set the `weblsp.tsserver.inlay.variable.enabled` property to `true` in your `settings.json`.
Supports Webpack, a static module bundler for modern JavaScript applications.
No description was specified by the owner of this plugin.

Provides the ability for Fleet workspaces to run natively within the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) environment.

Enables building, running, and analyzing code directly inside WSL.

WSL2 is required.
